summary: Not every adaptive interface needs a model or a cache.

points.text: A deterministic schema renderer is preferable when the schema already contains enough presentation semantics; model generation should earn its added cost and uncertainty.
Inline conversational components are naturally response-scoped and may be generated each time; caching is the default for substantial reusable views, not a rule for every fragment.
Input hashing is insufficient if undeclared prompt, model, component-library or theme changes can alter output; those dependencies must join the cache key or trigger an explicit migration.
